rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LORNA S. VILLANUEVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Notice of Hearing (Form DE-120) is required for this proceeding and Box 1 must be checked. (Probate Code Section 12201). 2) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 3) The filing of a report of status of administration shall include a petition requesting additional time for estate administration. For the Court to make an order, the report and petition must include a prayer specifying the relief sought (LRSF 14.35.J.2). 4) The instant petition requests that the court schedule a hearing on the amended second and final report and account of exectuor. In order for the court to give a hearing date, a distribution petition must be filed. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 03/11/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN N. NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 11/07/2025): 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver signed by each person entitled to notice. 3) Please provide facts in support of the decedent's domicile in San Francisco at the time of his death, rather than Alameda County. 4) Item 6b of the petition was checked, but Attachment 9 did not list the decedent's siblings or their issue. According to the devisee trust, Michele Sharp and Gregory Cohen are petitioner's niece and nephew (but not the decedent's). 5) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and their issue if any. 6) The marriage license issued to the decedent and petitioner in 2004 was voided by the California Supreme Court's ruling (see Lockyer v. City & County of San Francisco, 22 Cal.4th 1055) made in the same year. Were the decedent and petitioner married after same-sex marriage was subsequently legalized in California? 7) Is the correct lot number as part of the APN for the subject real property 045 (instead of 012)? 8) If the decedent and petitioner held title to the subject real property as joint tenants, the decedent's interest did not pass to petitioner. If property was held by spouses as joint tenants or community property with right of survivorship, the entire interest belongs to the surviving spouse by operation of law. See Grothe v. Cortlandt Corp. (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 1313, 1317 ("Nothing "passes" from the deceased joint tenant to the survivor; rather, the survivor takes from the instrument by which the joint tenancy was created."). 9) As the decedent left a pour-over will, his interest in the subject real property did not pass outright to petitioner individually as the surviving spouse. Property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will is subject to administration, even if the surviving spouse is the only trustee or beneficiary. See Probate Code section 13501(b). 10) If petitioner intended to request an order confirming the subject real property as an asset of the devisee trust without opening probate (as Schedule of Community Property Assets attached to the trust specifically listed the subject real property), a Heggstad petition must be filed under section 850(a)(3)(B) by opening a new trust case (starting with PTR) under the trust's name. As petitioner has an option to sign and record an affidavit of death of joint tenant to remove the decedent's name and transfer title to the entire property to petitioner as trustee, was this petition filed for the sole purpose of minimizing taxes (e.g., step-up basis applicable to community property including the survivor's share)? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROXIE E. HARRIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) An amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) of the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53(a)). 2) DE-121 is the mandatory form for notice of petition to administer estate, and the notice must indicate that an amended petition has been filed. 3) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 4) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 5) The proposed order lodged with the court does not mirror the content of the amended petition. Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 03/11/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
